title: argythamnia argothamnoides
intro: Argythamnia argothamnoides, also known as the West Indian arrowroot, is a type of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Argythamnia argothamnoides include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and potentially lower blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a poultice or applied topically as a cream or oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
